Carter M.Zinn, Esq., appeared for plaintiff & opposing party: Herbert Hoffman
Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Golden Gate Bridge Highway And Transportation District Motion To Compel Discovery Responses is moot in part as Plaintiff Catherine Dodd's responses to Defendant's Request For Production of Documents, Set One; Plaintiff Herbert Hoffman's Responses To Defendant's Special Interrogatories, Set One; and Plaintiff Catherine Dodd's Responses To Defendant's Special Interrogatories, Set One, have been served. Responsive documents shall be produced within 10 days of the date of hearing.
The motion is granted as to Defendant's Form Interrogatories, Sets One and Two, to Plaintiff Catherine Dodd; Defendant's Form Interrogatories, Set Two, to Plaintiff Herbert Hoffman; Supplemental Interrogatories to Plaintiff Herbert Hoffman; and Supplemental Request For Production Of Documents to Plaintiff Herbert Hoffman. Responses shall be made within 10 days of notice of this order.
Late opposition has been considered. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief From Waiver Of Objections To Deft'S Special Interrogatories, Form Interrogatories, And Requests For Productioin
Off Calendar Oct-16-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Carter M.Zinn, Esq., appeared for plaintiff & moving party: Herbert Hoffman
Kimon Manolius, Esq., appeared for Defendant & opposing party: Matthew Young

Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff Herbert Hoffman's Motion For Relief From Waiver Of Objections To Defendant's Special Interrogatories, Form Interrogatories, And Requests For Production is granted. However, counsel is advised that objections to discovery as vague and ambiguous and compound would not be sustained. As to any documents withheld pursuant to a claim of privilege, they must be identified with particularity at the time of production.
Defendant's objections numbered 1, 2, 4, 5, 6, 7, 9, and 11 are sustained. Defendant's objection numbered 3 is overruled. Defendant's objections numbered 8 and 10 are not considered for the truth of the matters asserted, but are admitted to establish that counsel had certain conversations. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
